content.subtitle: Use
rows: To convey what someone wanted to know
'**Did** you **finish** the report?' she asked him. → She asked him **if** he **had finished** the report.

rows.type: questions beginning with have, do or be

examples.direct: '**Have** you **met** her before?' he asked Tom.
examples.reported: He asked Tom **if** he **had met** her before.

examples.direct: '**Are** you **coming** to the party?' I asked Ben.
examples.reported: I asked Ben **if** he **was coming** to the party.

examples.direct: '**Can** you cook well?' she asked her friend.
examples.reported: She asked her friend **if** he **could** cook well.

examples.direct: '**Will** you lend me your notes?' I asked Karen.
examples.reported: I asked Karen **if** she **would** lend me her notes.

examples.direct: '**Shall** I bring something?' I asked Mum.
examples.reported: I asked Mum **if** I **should** bring something.

examples.direct: '**May** I use your phone?' Tim asked me.
examples.reported: Tim asked me **if** he **might** use my phone.

rows.type: questions beginning with a question word

examples.direct: '**What** time does the train arrive?' my sister asked me.
examples.reported: My sister asked me **what** time the train **arrived**.

examples.direct: '**Who** did you speak to?' asked Lisa.
'**Which** bus do you take?' Jack asked Sara.
'**When** will the results come out?' I asked.
examples.reported: I asked **when** the results **would** come out.

examples.direct: '**Why** are you upset?' my dad asked me.
examples.reported: My dad asked me **why** I **was** upset.

examples.direct: '**How long** did the journey take?' Sue asked Tom.
examples.reported: Sue asked Tom **how long** the journey **had taken**.
The teacher **told** us **not to be** late.

examples.direct: 'Will you help me move this table?' I asked Phil.
examples.reported: I **asked** Phil **to help** me move the table.

content.subtitle: Useful notes
items: In reported questions, **whether** can be used as an alternative to **if**.
She asked him whether he had finished the report.

content.subtitle: Be careful
items: Do not use question word order in reported questions.

items.correct: She asked where I had parked.

items.incorrect: She asked where had I parked.

sections.title: A: Original course practice
sections.instructions: Complete each original item using the target language from this lesson.

items.sentence: During the class project on Reported questions & orders, Mia wrote ...... to complete the model sentence.
items.answer: She asked me if I needed help.

items.sentence: In an original course practice item about Reported questions & orders, the best completion is ...... .
items.answer: Tom asked if they had finished the project.

items.sentence: Sara checked the course practice notes and added ...... to the example.
items.answer: My uncle asked me if I could drive.

items.sentence: The online lesson included a short sentence about Reported questions & orders: the missing part is ...... .
items.answer: He asked if she would come the next day.

items.sentence: During the school club on Reported questions & orders, Nina wrote ...... to complete the model sentence.
items.answer: Ana asked if she should call the manager.

items.sentence: In an original course practice item about Reported questions & orders, the best completion is ...... .
items.answer: Mum asked me if I had seen Ben.

sections.title: E: Original key word transformation practice
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it keeps the same meaning as the first.

items.sentence:
Mia began the class project before the lesson on Reported questions & orders. **IF**
The class project ...... before the lesson on Reported questions & orders.

Leo began the study group before the lesson on Reported questions & orders. **WHERE**
The study group ...... before the lesson on Reported questions & orders.

Sara began the exam workshop before the lesson on Reported questions & orders. **ASKED**
The exam workshop ...... before the lesson on Reported questions & orders.

Tom began the online lesson before the lesson on Reported questions & orders. **TOLD**
The online lesson ...... before the lesson on Reported questions & orders.

Nina began the school club before the lesson on Reported questions & orders. **WHEN**
The school club ...... before the lesson on Reported questions & orders.

Oscar began the revision session before the lesson on Reported questions & orders. **WHETHER**
The revision session ...... before the lesson on Reported questions & orders.
